REST API Design Best Practices: A well-designed REST API uses resource-oriented URLs (nouns, not verbs), standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE), and meaningful status codes to communicate outcomes. Versioning strategies—URL path versioning (/v1/), header versioning, or query parameter versioning—each have trade-offs in terms of cacheability and client complexity. Pagination via cursor-based or offset-based approaches is essential for collection endpoints returning large result sets. Consistent error response schemas with machine-readable error codes improve client-side error handling.
